text: Ethics in AI is increasingly important as technology becomes part of our daily lives. One major concern is bias, which occurs when AI systems reflect unfair assumptions from their training data, leading to discrimination. To ensure fairness, developers must carefully design algorithms that treat all users equally. Privacy is another key issue, as AI often collects personal data without clear consent. Transparency means companies should explain how their AI makes decisions, helping users trust the technology. Finally, responsible AI development requires ongoing testing and monitoring to prevent harm. By addressing these ethical challenges, we can create AI that benefits everyone while respecting human rights.
